keyMetrics.agentAndAiArr: Agentforce ARR $1.2B (+205% YoY); Agentforce + Data 360 ~$3.4B (+200%+, incl. ~$1.1B Informatica) — Q1 FY27
keyMetrics.remainingPerfObligation: cRPO $33.6B (+14% YoY); total RPO $67.9B (+11% YoY) — as of Apr 30, 2026
keyMetrics.capitalReturn: $50B buyback authorization; ~$24.9B repurchased in Q1 FY27; dividend $0.44/qtr

aiCommentary: Agentforce ARR crossed $1.2B, up 205% YoY — the fastest-scaling product line in company history and the centerpiece of the AI re-acceleration story. Combined Agentforce + Data 360 ARR reached ~$3.4B (+200%+ YoY), including ~$1.1B Informatica Cloud ARR. Bookings from premium SKUs (Agentforce One Edition, Agentforce for Apps) grew ~60% YoY. Usage signals underscored real consumption: 28.6T tokens processed (+152% QoQ) converted into 3.8B Agentic Work Units across Agentforce and Slack (+111% QoQ). Critically for durability, over 50% of Agentforce + Data 360 bookings came from existing customers expanding deployments — land-and-expand at higher consumption tiers rather than promotional land deals.
demandCommentary: Demand was framed as healthy and broadening. A record 98 deals over $1M new ACV closed in Q1, with Slack reportedly ~half of the $1M+ wins. cRPO of $33.6B (+14% YoY) outpaced reported revenue growth (+13%), a constructive forward-demand signal, and >50% of Agentforce + Data 360 bookings were existing-customer expansions. CFO Robin Washington explicitly guided to organic revenue acceleration in 2H FY27 (Sales, Service, Slack, Agentforce, Data 360) — implying 1H is the trough of the transition year. The Ramp Data demand signal corroborates dominance but flags the bear case: Salesforce holds 70.7% of CRM category card-spend (rank #1) yet category adoption slipped 1.2% YoY with a ~43% competitor switch rate.
